_comment: Distilled from PTCA, Systemic thrombolysis, CABG, IABP_Medco.pdf and PTCA, Systemic thrombolysis, CABG, IABP_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: M.Ch. or DNB (cardiovascular thoracic surgery) or equivalent
clinical_indications: Severe coronary artery disease (>70% stenosis in Two or Three vessel disease/>50% stenosis in left main coronary artery) not amenable to PTCA/ PCI, basis angiography and clinical parameters
Patient with diabetes mellitus and multi-vessel disease or left main disease (prefer CABG over PTCA/PCI)

clinical_thresholds.note: Stenosis in two or three of the main coronary artery
Stenosis in left main coronary artery
Documentary evidence revealing that the patient is not amenable to PTCA/ PCI

preauth.label: Cardiac enzymes (Troponins T/ I (if not available then CPK-MB)
Routine Biochemistry (Haemogram, urea, creatinine, electrolytes, sugar, fasting lipids, Liver function test, Urinalysis)

claim.label: Still image of the patient undergoing the procedure with date & time (With pic of the site of incision post procedure)
Cardiac enzymes (Troponins T/ I (if not available then CPK-MB)
Post op Chest X-ray showing sternal sutures

ppd_preauth.q: Consent duly signed by Patient & Cardiothoracic Surgeon- Yes
Stenosis of >/=70% in two or three of the main coronary artery- Yes
>50% stenosis in left main coronary artery- Yes
Documentary evidence revealing that the patient is not amenable to PTCA/ PCI- Yes

cpd_claim.q: Does the patient have severe coronary artery disease (>70% stenosis in Two or Three vessel disease/>50% stenosis in left main coronary artery) not amenable to PTCA/ PCI, basis angiography and clinical parameters?
Do OT notes detail steps of the Surgery undergone mentioning donor sites used for grafts and outcomes of surgery?
Is Post Op Chest X-Ray available showing Sternal sutures & Post procedure Clinical photograph showing chest scar?
Are the documents available to show monitoring of vitals (BP, Heart Rate), appropriate post-procedure care with continued medication, anti-ischemics, High dose Statins, Ace inhibitors, Beta blockers?
Is the Discharge summary available with discharge advise, medication and Life-style modification?

common_queries: Consent not signed by Patient and Cardiothoracic Surgeon.
Stenosis of main coronary artery documented below 70% in two or three vessels, or left main artery stenosis below 50%.
Documentary evidence not showing patient is not amenable to PTCA/PCI.
OT notes not detailing surgical steps, donor sites, or outcomes of surgery.
Post Op Chest X-Ray not showing Sternal sutures or clinical photograph not showing chest scar.
Discharge summary not showing proper monitoring of vitals, post-procedure care, medication, or lifestyle modification advice.
Coronary Angiography stills & reports (showing blockage) not uploaded at pre-auth.
Still image of the patient undergoing the procedure with date & time (with pic of the site of incision post procedure) not uploaded at claim.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Cardiac catheterization lab, Cardiac Care Unit & qualified Cardiologist (for PTCA/ PCI/ IABP)/ qualified cardiothoracic surgeon (for CABG)
clinical_key_pointers: Prefer CABG over PTCA/PCI if patient is having diabetes mellitus with multi-vessel disease or left main disease
